page: Google Cloud Platform c4-standard-24-lssd is a compute-focused virtual machine instance featuring 24 dedicated vCPUs and 90.0 GB of RAM. The underlying hardware is powered by an Intel Xeon 6985P-C processor operating at 2.3 GHz, configured with 12 physical cores and 2.0 threads per core. The CPU architecture is x86_64, supported by a cache hierarchy consisting of 48 KB L1d, 2 MB L2, and 504 MB L3 cache. This instance includes 1343 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, while offering zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark evaluations indicate top-tier single-core performance, with Geekbench single-core scores and single-threaded gzip compression rates placing in the top 10% of tested instances. Multi-core performance is strong, with Geekbench multi-core and bzip3 multi-threaded decompression scoring in the top 25%. However, multi-core stress-ng tests and Passmark CPU scores fall into the average category. Memory bandwidth tests for both small and large blocks also register average performance. For specialized workloads, the instance demonstrates strong static web serving throughput and prompt processing for small 135M LLM models, though text generation for large 70B models is poor, falling into the bottom 10%.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the c4-standard-24-lssd balances dedicated high-performance Intel Xeon compute resources with a substantial local NVMe SSD storage allocation, making it a resource-dense option for IOPS-demanding workloads without the overhead of external storage. The presence of advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) enhances its efficiency for vector and matrix operations. This server is suited for workloads such as medium-scale database hosting, software compilation, static web serving, and low-latency local data processing.
description: Google Cloud Platform c4-standard-24-lssd is an x86_64 virtual machine featuring 24 dedicated vCPUs, 90.0 GB of RAM, and 1343 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. Powered by an Intel Xeon 6985P-C processor running at 2.3 GHz with 12 physical cores, it delivers top-tier single-core performance, ranking in the top 10% for Geekbench single-core and single-threaded gzip compression. Multi-core capabilities are strong, supported by a large 504 MB L3 cache and advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and AMX. While memory bandwidth and multi-core stress tests perform at average levels, the instance excels in static web serving and small-model LLM text generation. Qualitatively, the bundled high-speed local storage provides excellent resource density for disk-heavy operations. This instance is well-suited for database hosting, software compilation, and localized data processing workloads.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-standard-24-lssd features 24 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 90 GB RAM, and 1343 GB local NVMe SSD. It delivers top-tier single-core performance for database and compute workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-standard-24-lssd is an x86_64 server with 24 vCPUs, 90 GB RAM, and 1343 GB NVMe SSD, optimized for compute and database tasks.
tagline: A dedicated Intel Xeon compute instance featuring high-speed local NVMe storage and top-tier single-core performance for demanding workloads.
bullet_points: 24 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 6985P-C processor at 2.3 GHz.
1343 GB of integrated local NVMe SSD storage.
Top-tier single-core performance in Geekbench and gzip compression benchmarks.
Equipped with Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) and AVX-512.
90.0 GB of system memory with a 504 MB L3 cache.
Suited for database hosting, software compilation, and static web serving.
